title: carex glauca subsp. dinarica
intro: Carex glauca subsp. dinarica, also known as Dinaric blue-sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex glauca sedge plant, which is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Carex glauca subsp. dinarica include its ability to help alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other related kidney problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate joint pain. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, allowing for a variety of applications in traditional medicine, including teas, poultices, and ointments.
